sections.content: The block at (56, 46) moved to (40, 40). Let me check the current state and continue moving blocks toward the blob.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['color'] not in ['B', 'W', 'c', 'G']:
        center = (sum(p[0] for p in n['boundary'])/len(n['boundary']),
                  sum(p[1] for p in n['boundary'])/len(n['boundary']))
        if center[0] > 5:
            print(f"Node: color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, center~({center[0]:.0f},{center[1]:.0f})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Hmm, the block at (56, 46) didn't move! It's still at (56, 46). The click at (40, 40) didn't move it. Maybe the block only moves when I click closer to it, or in a specific direction.
Let me reconsider the movement mechanic. Previously:
- Block at (36, 18) moved to (32, 22) when I clicked at (32, 22)
- Block at (32, 22) moved to (28, 26) when I clicked at (28, 26)
- Block at (28, 26) moved to (26, 28) when I clicked at (26, 28)
- Block at (26, 28) moved to (26, 30) when I clicked at (26, 30)
- Block at (40, 36) moved to (32, 34) when I clicked at (32, 34)
So the pattern is: clicking at a position moves the nearest block to that position. The block jumps directly to the clicked position.
But when I clicked at (40, 40), the block at (56, 46) didn't move. Maybe (40, 40) is too far from (56, 46), or maybe there's a movement range limit.
Let me try clicking closer to the block, like at (48, 44).

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try clicking closer to the block at (56, 46)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 48, 'col': 44}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
